text_en: Having considered the reports of the Special Committee to Investigate Israeli Practices Affecting the Human Rights of the Palestinian People and Other Arabs of the Occupied Territories 4 / and the reports of the Secretary-General on question 5 /,

text_fr: Prenant acte de la signature Ã  Washington, le 13 septembre 1993, par le Gouvernement de l'Etat d'IsraÃŦl et l'Organisation de libÃĐration de la Palestine, de la DÃĐclaration de principes sur des arrangements intÃĐrimaires d'autonomie, y compris ses annexes et le MÃĐmorandum d'accord y relatif 6 /,
text_en: Taking note of the signing in Washington, D.C., on 13 September 1993, by the Government of the State of Israel and the Palestine Liberation Organization, of the Declaration of Principles on Interim Self-Government Arrangements, including its Annexes and the Memorandum of Understanding 6 thereto,

items_fr: 1. FÃĐlicite le ComitÃĐ spÃĐcial chargÃĐ d'enquÃŠter sur les pratiques israÃĐliennes affectant les droits de l'homme du peuple palestinien et des autres Arabes des territoires occupÃĐs des efforts qu'il a faits pour s'acquitter des tÃĒches qu'elle lui avait confiÃĐes, ainsi que de l'objectivitÃĐ dont il a fait preuve;

4. Exprime l'espoir que, vu l'ÃĐvolution politique positive apparue rÃĐcemment, il sera mis immÃĐdiatement un terme Ã  la politique et aux pratiques en question;

5. Prie le ComitÃĐ spÃĐcial, en attendant qu'il soit entiÃĻrement mis fin Ã  l'occupation israÃĐlienne, de continuer Ã  enquÃŠter sur la politique et les pratiques israÃĐliennes dans le territoire palestinien occupÃĐ, y compris JÃĐrusalem, et dans les autres territoires arabes occupÃĐs par IsraÃŦl depuis 1967, de procÃĐder avec le ComitÃĐ international de la Croix-Rouge aux consultations voulues conformÃĐment Ã  son rÃĻglement pour sauvegarder le bien-ÃŠtre et les droits de l'hommme des peuples des territoires occupÃĐs, et de prÃĐsenter au SecrÃĐtaire gÃĐnÃĐral un rapport Ã  ce sujet le plus tÃīt possible, et, par la suite, chaque fois qu'il y aura lieu;
6. Prie ÃĐgalement le ComitÃĐ spÃĐcial de prÃĐsenter rÃĐguliÃĻrement au SecrÃĐtaire gÃĐnÃĐral des rapports pÃĐriodiques sur la situation dans le territoire palestinien occupÃĐ;
items_en: 1. Commends the Special Committee to Investigate Israeli Practices Affecting the Human Rights of the Palestinian People and Other Arabs of the Occupied Territories for its efforts to carry out the tasks entrusted to it by the General Assembly and for its objectivity;
2. Demands that Israel cooperate with the Special Committee in the implementation of its mandate;

3. Deplores the policies and practices of Israel, which violate the human rights of the Palestinian people and other Arabs of the occupied territories, as indicated in the reports of the Special Committee for the period under review; 4/
4. Expresses the hope that, in the light of recent positive political developments, the policy and practices in question will be brought to an immediate end;

5. Requests the Special Committee, pending the complete cessation of the Israeli occupation, to continue to investigate Israeli policies and practices in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 1967, to consult with the International Committee of the Red Cross in accordance with its rules of procedure in order to safeguard the well-being and rights of the people of the occupied territories, and to submit a report thereon to the Secretary-General as soon as possible, and thereafter whenever appropriate;
6. Also requests the Special Committee to submit periodic reports to the Secretary-General on a regular basis on the situation in the Occupied Palestinian Territory;

items_fr: 7. Prie en outre le ComitÃĐ spÃĐcial de continuer Ã  enquÃŠter sur le traitement des prisonniers dans le territoire palestinien occupÃĐ, y compris JÃĐrusalem, et dans les autres territoires arabes occupÃĐs par IsraÃŦl depuis 1967;
8. Prie le SecrÃĐtaire gÃĐnÃĐral :

a) De mettre Ã  la disposition du ComitÃĐ spÃĐcial tous les moyens nÃĐcessaires pour qu'il puisse enquÃŠter sur la politique et les pratiques israÃĐliennes visÃĐes dans la prÃĐsente rÃĐsolution, y compris les moyens dont le ComitÃĐ aura besoin pour se rendre dans les territoires occupÃĐs;
b) De continuer Ã  fournir au ComitÃĐ spÃĐcial le personnel supplÃĐmentaire dont il pourra avoir besoin pour accomplir ses tÃĒches;
c) De transmettre rÃĐguliÃĻrement aux Etats Membres les rapports pÃĐriodiques visÃĐs au paragraphe 6 ci-dessus;

d) D'assurer la plus large diffusion possible aux rapports du ComitÃĐ spÃĐcial et Ã  ses activitÃĐs et conclusions, par tous les moyens dont dispose le DÃĐpartement de l'information du SecrÃĐtariat et, si nÃĐcessaire, de rÃĐimprimer les rapports du ComitÃĐ spÃĐcial qui sont ÃĐpuisÃĐs;
e) De lui prÃĐsenter un rapport, Ã  sa quarante-neuviÃĻme session, sur l'accomplissement des tÃĒches qu'elle lui confie par la prÃĐsente rÃĐsolution;

9. DÃĐcide d'inscrire Ã  l'ordre du jour provisoire de sa quaranteneuviÃĻme session la question intitulÃĐe "Rapport du ComitÃĐ spÃĐcial chargÃĐ d'enquÃŠter sur les pratiques israÃĐliennes affectant les droits de l'homme du peuple palestinien et des autres Arabes des territoires occupÃĐs".
items_en: 7. Further requests the Special Committee to continue to investigate the treatment of prisoners in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 1967;
8. Requests the Secretary-General:

(a) To provide the Special Committee with all the means necessary for it to investigate the Israeli policies and practices referred to in the present resolution, including the means necessary for the Committee to visit the occupied territories;
(b) To continue to provide the Special Committee with the additional staff it may require to carry out its tasks;
(c) To transmit regularly to Member States the periodic reports referred to in paragraph 6 above;

(d) To ensure the widest possible dissemination of the reports of the Special Committee and its activities and conclusions, through all means available to the Department of Public Information of the Secretariat and, where necessary, to reprint the reports of the Special Committee that have been exhausted;
(e) To submit a report to the General Assembly at its forty-ninth session on the fulfilment of the tasks entrusted to it by the Assembly under the present resolution;

9. Decides to include in the provisional agenda of its forty-ninth session the item entitled "Report of the Special Committee to Investigate Israeli Practices Affecting the Human Rights of the Palestinian People and Other Arabs of the Occupied Territories".

text_en: Stressing the need for Israel, the occupying Power, to comply strictly with its obligations under international law,

text_en: Page 4

items_fr: 1. RÃĐaffirme que la Convention de GenÃĻve relative Ã  la protection des personnes civiles en temps de guerre, du 12 aoÃŧt 1949 1 /, s'applique au territoire palestinien occupÃĐ, y compris JÃĐrusalem, et aux autres territoires arabes occupÃĐs par IsraÃŦl depuis 1967;

2. Enjoint Ã  IsraÃŦl de reconnaÃŪtre l'applicabilitÃĐ de jure de la Convention dans le territoire palestinien occupÃĐ, y compris JÃĐrusalem, et les autres territoires arabes occupÃĐs par IsraÃŦl depuis 1967, et d'en respecter scrupuleusement les dispositions;

3. Exhorte tous les Etats parties Ã  la Convention, agissant conformÃĐment Ã  l'article premier commun aux quatre Conventions de GenÃĻve 7 /, Ã  tout mettre en oeuvre pour en faire respecter les dispositions par IsraÃŦl, Puissance occupante, dans le territoire palestinien occupÃĐ, y compris JÃĐrusalem, et les autres territoires arabes occupÃĐs par IsraÃŦl depuis 1967;

items_en: 1. Reaffirms that the Geneva Convention relative to the Protection of Civilian Persons in Time of War, of 12 August 1949, 1 / applies to the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 1967;

2. Urges Israel to recognize the de jure applicability of the Convention in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 1967, and to comply scrupulously with its provisions;

3. Urges all States parties to the Convention, in accordance with article 1 common to the four Geneva Conventions 7 /, to make every effort to ensure respect for its provisions by Israel, the occupying Power, in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 1967;
4. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the General Assembly at its forty-ninth session on the implementation of the present resolution.

text_en: Gravely concerned also about the situation in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 1967 as a result of measures and actions taken by Israel, the occupying Power, to change its legal status, geographical character and demographic composition,

text_en: Concerned about the dangerous situation created by the acts of the armed settlers illegally settled in the occupied territory,

text_fr: Convaincue de l'effet positif qu'exerce la prÃĐsence internationale dans le territoire palestinien occupÃĐ pour ce qui est d'assurer le respect des dispositions de la Convention de GenÃĻve relative Ã  la protection des personnes civiles en temps de guerre, du 12 aoÃŧt 1949 1 /,

text_en: Reaffirming that the Convention applies to the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 1967,

items_fr: Se fÃĐlicitant de la signature Ã  Washington, le 13 septembre 1993, par le Gouvernement de l'Etat d'IsraÃŦl et l'Organisation de libÃĐration de la Palestine, de la DÃĐclaration de principes sur des arrangements intÃĐrimaires d'autonomie, y compris ses annexes et le MÃĐmorandum d'accord y relatif 6 /,

1. ConsidÃĻre que toutes les mesures et dÃĐcisions prises par IsraÃŦl, Puissance occupante, dans le territoire palestinien occupÃĐ, y compris JÃĐrusalem, qui contreviennent aux dispositions de la Convention de GenÃĻve relative Ã  la protection des personnes civiles en temps de guerre, du 12 aoÃŧt 1949 1 /, et aux rÃĐsolutions pertinentes du Conseil de sÃĐcuritÃĐ, sont illÃĐgales et sans valeur, et exige qu'IsraÃŦl cesse immÃĐdiatement de prendre des mesures ou dÃĐcisions de cette nature;
2. Enjoint Ã  IsraÃŦl, Puissance occupante, de faciliter le retour de tous les Palestiniens expulsÃĐs du territoire palestinien occupÃĐ depuis 1967;
3. Demande Ã  IsraÃŦl, Puissance occupante, d'accÃĐlÃĐrer la libÃĐration de tous les Palestiniens dÃĐtenus ou emprisonnÃĐs arbitrairement;

4. Demande ÃĐgalement le plein respect par la Puissance occupante de toutes les libertÃĐs fondamentales du peuple palestinien, telles que la libertÃĐ de l'enseignement, qui comprend le libre fonctionnement des ÃĐcoles, universitÃĐs et autres ÃĐtablissements d'enseignement;

5. RÃĐaffirme que les colonies de peuplement israÃĐliennes ÃĐtablies dans le territoire palestinien occupÃĐ, y compris JÃĐrusalem, et les autres territoires arabes occupÃĐs par IsraÃŦl depuis 1967 sont illÃĐgales et font obstacle Ã  la paix;

items_en: Welcoming the signing in Washington, D.C., on 13 September 1993 by the Government of the State of Israel and the Palestine Liberation Organization, of the Declaration of Principles on Interim Self-Government Arrangements, including its Annexes and the Memorandum of Understanding 6 thereto,

1. Recognizes that all measures and actions taken by Israel, the occupying Power, in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, that contravene the provisions of the Geneva Convention relative to the Protection of Civilian Persons in Time of War, of 12 August 1949, 1/ and the relevant resolutions of the Security Council are illegal and of no value, and demands that Israel immediately cease taking such measures or decisions;
2. Urges Israel, the occupying Power, to facilitate the return of all Palestinians expelled from the Palestinian territory occupied since 1967;
3. Calls upon Israel, the occupying Power, to accelerate the release of all Palestinians arbitrarily detained or imprisoned;

4. Also calls for full respect by the occupying Power for all the fundamental freedoms of the Palestinian people, such as the freedom of education, which includes the free functioning of schools, universities and other educational institutions;
5. Reaffirms that Israeli settlements in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 1967 are illegal and an obstacle to peace;
6. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the General Assembly at its forty-ninth session on the implementation of the present resolution.

3. ConsidÃĻre que toutes les mesures et dÃĐcisions lÃĐgislatives et administratives qui ont ÃĐtÃĐ prises ou seront prises par IsraÃŦl, Puissance occupante, pour modifier le caractÃĻre et le statut juridique du Golan syrien
items_en: Bearing in mind Security Council resolution 237 (1967) of 14 June 1967,

1. Condemns Israel, the occupying Power, for its refusal to observe the resolutions concerning the occupied Syrian Golan, in particular Security Council resolution 497 (1981), in which, inter alia, the Council decided that Israel's decision to impose its laws, jurisdiction and administration on the occupied Syrian Arab Golan was null and void and without international legal effect and demanded that Israel, the occupying Power, immediately rescind its decision;

2. Also condemns the persistence of Israel in changing the physical character, demographic composition, institutional structure and legal status of the occupied Syrian Golan, and in particular condemns the establishment of settlements;
3. Recognizes that all legislative and administrative measures and actions taken or to be taken by Israel, the occupying Power, to change the character and legal status of the Syrian Golan

text_en: are null and void, are in flagrant violation of international law and the Geneva Convention relative to the Protection of Civilian Persons in Time of War, of 12 August 1949, 1/ and have no legal effect;

DÃĐnonce les tentatives faites par IsraÃŦl pour imposer par la force aux citoyens syriens du Golan syrien occupÃĐ la nationalitÃĐ israÃĐlienne et des cartes d'identitÃĐ israÃĐliennes et demande Ã  IsraÃŦl de renoncer Ã  ses mesures rÃĐpressives contre la population du Golan syrien occupÃĐ;

7. Prie le SecrÃĐtaire gÃĐnÃĐral de lui rendre compte, Ã  sa quaranteneuviÃĻme session, de l'application de la prÃĐsente rÃĐsolution.
items_en: 4. Condemns Israel's attempts to impose Israeli nationality and Israeli identity cards on Syrian citizens in the occupied Syrian Golan by force, and calls upon Israel to renounce its repressive measures against the population of the occupied Syrian Golan;
5. Deplores Israel ' s violations of the Convention;
6. Calls once again upon Member States not to recognize any of the legislative and administrative measures or decisions referred to above;
7. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the General Assembly at its forty-ninth session on the implementation of the present resolution.
